is wired to two buttons in the UI but has no implementation anywhere in the file.
is declared at line 90, confirming the method was planned

(line 6386). The guard field is already declared. Skeleton:
If the editor body exists in another source file from the modular refactor (v0.775), check that file for the implementation and confirm only the entry-point call was dropped from
Bug 2 — Supporting types are referenced but not defined in this file
The filename parser subsystem depends on several types and service members that are used throughout
but have no definition visible in this file. This will cause compile errors if the definitions were lost during the modular refactor.
not called yet — needed by editor

These types were all introduced in v0.785 as part of centralizing filename parsing behind a shared service. The v0.787 entry specifically mentions fixing “Filename Rules editor bindings by converting the filename parsing models to bindable CLR properties” — this implies the editor form itself exists, but the types may live in one of the source files extracted during the modular refactor (v0.775).
If the types live in another source file:
confirm they were not accidentally excluded from the build. The partial class pattern used throughout the project means they should compile together automatically if included.
as CLR properties (per v0.787 fix note).
this method will definitely be needed once
is implemented and the user can edit/save rules. Make sure it’s present on
before wiring up the editor save button.
check all source files from the v0.775 modular split. If found, confirm it is included in the build. If missing, write it using the skeleton in Bug 1.
confirm all four are defined and included in the build.
one in the main settings header (line 219) and one in the Library browser filter bar (line 4780). Both should open the same singleton editor window.
v0.787 fixed a binding regression. Confirm built-in rules and custom DB-backed rules both render correctly in the editor grids after the fix.
